scenario: Habitat self-use separates ordinary SwiftPM validation from release artifact verification
contextMode: fresh self-scan plus command policy review before Git publication
fixtureState: Habitat source repository observed through a fresh local scan; raw report output and local paths are omitted.
taskSummary: Choose the first validation command for ordinary development while release artifact scripts are also documented.
relevantHabitatSignals: Project files selected SwiftPM as the ordinary workflow.
Generated context preferred swift test and swift build.
Generated context recorded release/artifact validation as a separate fact.
Generated context explicitly kept the artifact script for release prep or artifact verification.
The artifact script was not promoted into policy preferredCommands.

askFirst: Git/GitHub mutation before publishing the Habitat-side fixture
doNot: treat ./scripts/build_release_artifacts.sh as the first ordinary validation command
move released v0.6.0 tag or GitHub Release assets
expand into a broad setup, lint, smoke, package, or CI taxonomy
reviewFirst: command_policy.md before staging, committing, or pushing the Habitat-side change

withHabitatContext.firstProposedAction: Use swift test for ordinary local validation and leave the artifact script for release-prep checks.

evidenceNormalizationImplication: The existing ValidationCommandClaim purpose boundary is enough for this slice; no broader validation taxonomy is justified.

verdict.reason: Fresh Habitat context preserved swift test as ordinary validation while keeping artifact generation as release-prep context.
verdict.followUpImprovement: Only add more validation-purpose categories after repeated fixtures show setup, lint, smoke, package, or CI distinctions changing the first command.

artifactImprovementIfFailed: If agents run release artifact scripts for ordinary validation, tighten generated context wording before adding a broad taxonomy.
